title: microlaena avenacea
intro: Microlaena avenacea, also known as Swain's grass or Rice grass, is a perennial grass native to the southeastern regions of Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Microlaena avenacea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory conditions, and aid in wound healing. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
